name: AMPK and metabolic modulators field change check for June 7, 2026

scope.question: What changed for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators since the 2026-06-01 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a field change check from the 2026-06-01T13:01:41.273Z AMPK baseline publication through June 7, 2026. The local trial audit flagged ANTHEM as completed with no posted results and four stale or missing registry checks; current re-checks found ANTHEM unchanged with no results, the metformin frailty-prevention trial and MILES unchanged with ClinicalTrials.gov results posted, MET-PREVENT completed with ISRCTN results already posted before this delta window, and METFORAGING still listed as ongoing in the EU register while the peer-reviewed result remains the public evidence source. PubMed and registry searches found no new result-bearing AMPK/metformin/berberine/acarbose aging evidence. Close items included a metformin-exercise systematic review, the DEMFOS metformin frailty protocol and no-results registry, an observational RESORT gerotherapeutic-drug mortality association, a yeast metformin longevity mechanism paper, a metformin geroprotector review, and a recruiting metformin MCI registry with no results. None changes the current tentative metformin-led human biomarker signal, the mixed/null older-adult function boundary, safety/tolerability framing, momentum, or review priority.

materiality_decision.rationale: The delta search did not find newly posted trial results, a new treated-human aging result, a correction or retraction, or a category-level source gap that would alter the public outlook or support map. The closest new protocol and registry items are no-results trial context, while the review and observational records reinforce existing boundaries without adding causal aging evidence.

search_log.summary: The audit found five registry-linked interventional studies, all with trial_details. ANTHEM was the only completed no-results record. MET-PREVENT, METFORAGING, NCT02570672, and MILES had posted results recorded locally but missing or stale registry checks.

search_log.query: Known NCT records NCT04264897, NCT02570672, and NCT02432287
search_log.summary: NCT04264897 remains completed, last update posted 2026-04-23, with 2025-03-12 actual completion, 101 actual enrollment, and no registry results. NCT02570672 remains completed, last update posted 2025-08-06, with 2024-02-07 actual completion and registry results posted. NCT02432287 remains completed, last update posted 2021-05-21, with registry results posted.

The PDF export lists MET-PREVENT as completed, no longer recruiting, with final enrollment 72, completion date 2023-08-31, results/protocol/basic-results outputs, and last edited 2026-04-28. That is pre-window and matches the existing public MET-PREVENT null/tolerability boundary.

The EU register page is reachable and still lists METFORAGING as ongoing with the phase II metformin-versus-placebo biological-aging protocol in older adults with HIV. The peer-reviewed METFORAGING result is already the public evidence source, so the registry status mismatch does not change the outlook.

search_log.query: metformin AND (aging OR older adult OR frailty OR geroscience OR epigenetic age); AMPK AND aging terms; berberine AND aging terms; (metformin OR berberine OR acarbose) AND aging/frailty/sarcopenia terms
search_log.summary: The broad registry screen returned close no-results context, especially NCT04221750 DEMFOS and NCT07395960 metformin in elderly overweight/obesity with MCI. Other AMPK, berberine, and acarbose hits were disease-specific, non-aging, pre-window, or no-results records and did not alter public interpretation.

search_log.query: metformin aging; AMPK aging; berberine aging; metformin/berberine/acarbose metabolic-modulator aging terms, publication dates 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: The closest records were PMID 42139118, a metformin-exercise systematic review/meta-analysis; PMID 42009100, the DEMFOS protocol; PMID 41713660, an observational RESORT medication-use and mortality analysis; PMID 41707917, a yeast chronological-aging mechanism paper; and PMID 41832079, a metformin geroprotector review. None supplied new causal treated-human aging evidence or a public-stage-changing result.

search_log.query: berberine and AMPK same-window hits; broader metabolic-modulator same-window hits
search_log.summary: Berberine and AMPK hits included comments, diabetes/metabolic-memory reviews, toxic-metal or disease models, and non-aging clinical contexts. No same-window berberine, acarbose, or direct AMPK modulator aging trial result was found.

trial_watch_checks.summary: ANTHEM remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section after its 2026-04-23 registry update. This keeps the no-results watch item unchanged.
The frailty-prevention registry remains completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results and no update since 2025-08-06. No public change is needed.

MILES remains completed with ClinicalTrials.gov results posted and no recent registry movement; the public evidence layer already uses PMID 29383869 for the small older-adult transcriptomic/biomarker signal.
The ISRCTN export lists MET-PREVENT as completed with result outputs and a 2026-04-28 last edit. The result is already represented by PMID 40147475 and does not need a same-window public refresh.

The EU register still lists the METFORAGING protocol as ongoing, while the peer-reviewed METFORAGING pilot result is already public as PMID 42023167. This registry discrepancy does not alter the track outlook.

excluded_sources.title: Influence of metformin on exercise metabolism and capacity: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
excluded_sources.reason: Review/meta-analysis of metformin and exercise metabolism through January 2025. It reports lactate accumulation and possible dose/duration/participant interactions, reinforcing the existing exercise-adaptation boundary without adding a new aging intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: Geroprotective effects of diet and exercise plus metformin in frail older veterans with obesity: The DEMFOS randomized trial protocol.
excluded_sources.reason: Clinical trial protocol for a six-month metformin plus lifestyle trial in 114 older veterans with obesity and frailty. It is relevant watch context but has no outcome data.

excluded_sources.title: Diet and Exercise Plus Metformin to Treat Frailty in Obese Seniors
excluded_sources.reason: Completed DEMFOS registry with 114 actual enrollment, 2025-07-31 actual completion, and no posted ClinicalTrials.gov results. It was updated before the AMPK delta window and does not change the public evidence map.

excluded_sources.title: Use of potential gerotherapeutic drugs and mortality in geriatric rehabilitation inpatients: RESORT.
excluded_sources.reason: Observational medication-use analysis in geriatric rehabilitation inpatients. Metformin use was associated with lower one-year mortality after comorbidity adjustment, but there was no causal randomization, no physical-function association, and no AMPK-specific aging intervention claim.

excluded_sources.title: Metformin-induced longevity is associated with retrotransposon dynamics in yeast chronological aging.
excluded_sources.reason: Yeast chronological-aging mechanism paper linking metformin to chromatin and retrotransposon dynamics. It is preclinical mechanism context and does not change the human biomarker or function read.

excluded_sources.title: Revisiting metformin as a geroprotector.
excluded_sources.reason: Review/editorial context without new primary metformin aging results.

excluded_sources.title: Treatment of type 2 diabetes among medicare beneficiaries with and without Alzheimer's disease: A retrospective cohort study.
excluded_sources.reason: Older-adult metformin exposure appears only as diabetes treatment-pattern context in Medicare beneficiaries with Alzheimer's disease. It is not a geroscience intervention or aging-outcome result.

excluded_sources.title: The Efficacy and Safety of Metformin Intervention in Elderly Overweight or Obesity With Mild Cognitive Impairment
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting metformin trial in older overweight or obese adults with MCI, with estimated 2026-09-30 completion and no results. It is watch context rather than a same-window evidence delta.

excluded_sources.title: Comment on: Berberine suppresses NLRP3 inflammasome through AMPK pathway in atrial fibrillation.
excluded_sources.reason: Comment-level atrial-fibrillation context returned by the berberine/AMPK search; not an aging result.

excluded_sources.title: AMPK as a central regulator of metabolic memory and beyond in diabetes complications: mechanisms and therapeutic targets.
excluded_sources.reason: Diabetes-complications review context rather than a direct AMPK/metabolic-modulator aging intervention result.

notes: Triage selected this as the next ready surveillance item after the senomorphics update and public rollup were committed.
The delta window is anchored to publication event publish-ampk-and-metabolic-modulators-bootstrap-2026-06-01-2026-06-01t13-01-41-273z.
No coverage assessment exists yet for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators; this ordinary surveillance pass did not discover a material category-level gap requiring immediate coverage repair.
DEMFOS/NCT04221750 is relevant no-results metformin frailty context but was not staged because it adds no result-bearing public evidence and was not a same-window registry activity change.

The public boundary remains unchanged: metformin supplies the main human biomarker signal, older-adult function and frailty results remain mixed or null, and berberine/acarbose do not have new human aging results in this delta window.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for ANTHEM posted results, DEMFOS posted results, NCT07395960 results, or other result-bearing AMPK/metabolic-modulator aging updates.

Consider a separate AMPK coverage assessment if trial-watch completeness becomes a maintenance priority, especially for whether DEMFOS should be represented in the public trial layer before results are posted.

Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes the stage, evidence weight, momentum, blockers, safety/tolerability framing, human target engagement, functional outcomes, or AMPK/metabolic-modulator translation boundary.
